label: SES-014 close-out — process schema-design conversation (workstream conversation #3 of 4)

session.title: process schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #3
session.summary: Third of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream. Produced the process schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md, 499 lines) defining the process entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Architectural decisions resolved into five DEC records (DEC-055 through DEC-059). Inherited the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 and applied by SES-013 (parent-prefix field naming, {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming, soft-3-letter prefix posture with explicit deviation clause, rejection-via-soft-delete) and explicitly deviated on one structural point: process does not have a status field in v0.4. The deviation rationale — domain/entity use status for engagement-scope lifecycle, process uses process_classification for that role, and a definitional-completeness lifecycle field becomes meaningful only when v0.5+ adds Phase 3 detail levels — is documented in spec section 3.4.1. Registered process_hands_off_to_process as the second formal cross-entity vocabulary entry in the methodology workstream, directional (source = producer, target = consumer). Deferred process-to-entity touches entirely (no vocab registration in v0.4) under PI-005. One new planning item authored (PI-011 future scalar implementation-priority field). Mid-conversation, identified working-cadence calibration issues with the conversation's own dialog mode and produced two paste-ready prompts for a separate Claude.ai session to update userPreferences and project-level instructions: a primary prompt establishing detail / architecture / prototype mode definitions, and a follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template (eight elements: plain-language question, concrete example, options with concrete behavior, why it matters, cost, recommendation, follow-on detail, decision request). The preferences-revision work is queued for a separate session before the next CRMBuilder conversation. Successor conversation: crm_candidate schema design.
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation that produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md on 05-12-26. Third of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream established by SES-011. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the process entity type schema for v2's storage layer. A 'process' in the methodology sense is a sequence of work the organization does to advance its mission — Client Intake, Mentor Onboarding, Annual Dues Cycle, etc. — the things that get full Process Documents in Phase 3 of the evolved methodology and that constitute the Prioritized Backbone in Phase 1. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md — the complete schema specification per the template in methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (first predecessor, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.0 (second predecessor, 452 lines, source of references-decomposition pattern and master-pane-column-deferral pattern); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from domain, applied); close-out-payloads/ses_013.json (DEC-050..054 + PI-009/010 from entity, payload exists but not yet applied per db-export snapshot — numbers in this SES-014 payload assume SES-013 lands first); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (section 3.5 Part C process surfacing; section 4.1 between-session drafting with workability check; section 7.3 Prioritized Backbone output spec); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md (Principle 3 priority-at-process-level; Principle 7 priority-not-locked; Phase 1 / Phase 3 boundary).

Architectural questions resolved:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Adopted PROC (four letters) invoking domain.md's soft-3-letter posture explicit deviation clause. PRC rejected for ambiguity (reads as typo, no natural disambiguation). PR collides with public relations and is two letters. P too short. No collision with DEC/SES/RSK/PI/TOP/REF/CHR/STA/DOM/ENT. → DEC-055.

Q2 — Priority vs. status: two fields or one. The kickoff prompt's working set (candidate / confirmed / deprecated / deferred) conflated two orthogonal concepts: methodology priority classification (per Principle 3) and propose-verify record lifecycle. After clarifying that the user reframed the lifecycle question as definitional-completeness rather than existence-verification ("in Phase 1 a process is identified and an overview provided; in later phases the process is refined / detailed / confirmed to be complete"), resolved that v0.4 schema has only the identified level and therefore no status field — a status field with one value is no field at all. The methodology's deferred semantic that status carries for domain and entity is captured for process by classification=deferred instead; overloading both fields would create field-collision ambiguity. Rejection continues to be handled by soft-delete per cross-spec precedent. → contributes to DEC-056 (no-status deviation) and DEC-057 (classification field).

Q3 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Three substantive content fields: process_purpose (required, one-sentence — explicitly produced in Phase 1 7.3 output for backbone processes), process_classification_rationale (optional — priority-test answer for mission-critical; one-line reason for supporting and deferred), process_notes (optional, internal scratchpad). No separate process_description (purpose carries the work-content meaning). No process_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-005). No storage-level length caps. Engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness on process_name. Direct FK to domain via process_domain_identifier (required). → contributes to DEC-056.

Q4 — Process classification: term and values. User pushed back on the working name 'priority' because conventional priorities are scalar (Critical/High/Medium/Low) while the methodology's three buckets (mission-critical / supporting / deferred) are a fused classification mixing importance, scope, and role rather than a scalar. Adopted process_classification as the field name and unclassified | mission_critical | supporting | deferred as the enum values, with unclassified default reflecting the Session-1-capture-before-classification state. One-way out of unclassified; free movement among the three classified values per Principle 7. Future scalar implementation-priority field deferred and tracked as a new PI. → DEC-057. Future scalar tracked as PI-011.

Q5 — Domain affiliation mechanism. Direct FK column on process table (process_domain_identifier), not references-table edge. Cardinality settled at the methodology level (many-to-one). No vocab.py registration — the references vocab governs the references table only, and a direct FK doesn't produce references rows. Domain.md section 3.3.2's anticipated relationship name (process_belongs_to_domain) stays informational only. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q6 — Process-to-process connections: directional or undirected, name. Directional, per the methodology's handoff language in Phase 1 guide section 4.1 ("Process A hands off to Process B if A produces records or state that B requires") and section 7.3 output (handoffs to/from other backbone processes — both sides named distinctly). Source = producer; target = consumer. Relationship kind name: process_hands_off_to_process. Registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and _kinds_for_pair; Alembic migration extends CHECK constraint. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q7 — Process-to-entity touches in v0.4: defer entirely or register vocab now. Deferred entirely. PI-005 (process schema growth beyond Phase 1 thin shape) already tracks this. Pre-committing to a coarse vocab name (process_touches_entity) risks ossifying the wrong name when Phase 3 may need finer-grained kinds (read / create / update / delete). The spec's anticipated-inbound-relationships table (section 3.3.2) lists process_touches_entity as v0.5+ vocab without registration. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q8 — Process name uniqueness: per-(domain, name) or engagement-global. Initially proposed per-(domain, name) to permit natural client-language names like 'Recruit' in both MR and CR domains. User pushed back asking what documentation would show for a per-domain unique name and applied the principle 'if there could ever be user confusion, then we should go engagement specific.' The handoff lists in Phase 1 output documents reference process names alone without domain context, creating ambiguity risk under per-domain uniqueness. Adopted engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness mirroring domain/entity precedent; consultants use suffixed names (Mentor Recruit, Client Recruit) as CBM already does today. → contributes to DEC-056.

Q9 — API surface, UI defaults, acceptance criteria. Standard endpoint set with no deviations. Decomposed reference handling for handoffs (no inline-handoff convenience endpoints), matching entity.md decomposed posture for affiliations. UI: Methodology sidebar group position #3 (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates); master pane columns Identifier / Name / Classification / Updated (Domain column deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007, mirroring entity.md's posture); detail pane renders bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process references in separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections; create-dialog handoff flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as v0.4-build decision matching the open question on entity affiliations. 15 testable acceptance criteria. → DEC-059.

Mid-conversation working-cadence calibration. Identified that the conversation's own dialog mode (per-section permission requests on every architectural sub-question, then over-correcting to flat-weight consolidated proposals without per-decision depth) was not serving the user. Produced two paste-ready prompts to be run in a separate session to update userPreferences, project-level instructions, and kickoff-prompt templates: (1) a primary prompt defining three named modes (detail / architecture / prototype) with cadence rules and a default mode declaration mechanism; (2) a follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template — eight elements: plain-language question, concrete example, each option with concrete behavior, why it matters, cost of recommendation honestly named, recommendation with rationale, follow-on detail separated, decision request with named options. The two prompts together provide the long-term fix; the user runs them in a separate session before the next CRMBuilder conversation.
session.artifacts_produced:
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md (the process methodology entity schema specification, v1.0, 499 lines).

Five decisions:
- DEC-055: process identifier prefix and format (PROC, four letters via soft-3-letter posture deviation)
- DEC-056: process field inventory under v0.4 scope (three content fields, no description-distinct-from-purpose, engagement-global name uniqueness, no status field deviation)
- DEC-057: process_classification field (four-value enum, unclassified default, one-way unclassified gate with free movement among classified values, replaces working name process_priority)
- DEC-058: process relationship architecture (direct FK for domain, references-entity directional edge for process_hands_off_to_process, defer process-to-entity)
- DEC-059: process API surface, UI defaults, acceptance criteria (standard endpoints, position #3 in Methodology sidebar, Domain column deferred to v0.5+, 15 acceptance criteria)

One new planning item:
- PI-011: future scalar implementation-priority field for ranking within classification buckets

Two paste-ready prompts produced as conversation content (not committed files; queued for use in a separate Claude.ai session): preferences-revision primary prompt defining detail / architecture / prototype modes; preferences-revision follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template.
session.in_flight_at_end:
Two parallel queued workstreams.

Main workstream: next conversation is crm_candidate schema design (fourth and final schema-design conversation in the methodology entity schema-design workstream). Kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md. The crm_candidate conversation inherits the conventions established and applied across SES-012 / SES-013 / SES-014 (parent-prefix field naming, {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming, soft-3-letter prefix posture with explicit four-letter deviation when warranted, rejection-via-soft-delete, no-archived, decomposed reference handling, engagement-global case-insensitive name uniqueness). After crm_candidate completes, the v0.4-build-planning conversation takes all four schema specs as input and produces the v0.4 PRD, implementation plan, and slice build prompts. Cross-spec consistency check runs at the start of the v0.4-build-planning conversation, verifying no vocab-kind name collisions and that conventions are applied consistently.

Parallel workstream: preferences-revision session queued. The user will run two paste-ready prompts in a separate Claude.ai session before opening the crm_candidate conversation: (1) primary prompt defining detail / architecture / prototype modes with cadence rules and default-mode declaration mechanism; (2) follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template. Output: revised userPreferences, project-level instructions, and kickoff-prompt template additions. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md file should be updated with an 'Operating mode: architecture' (or 'prototype') line at the top once the preferences-revision lands, so the cadence is explicit before the next conversation opens.

decisions.title: process identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: The process methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. Per domain.md section 3.1 the soft-3-letter posture preserves flexibility for downstream methodology entity types to deviate to four letters when the three-letter form is ambiguous; the example given was PROC over PRC for clarity.
decisions.decision: Adopt PROC as the identifier prefix for the process methodology entity. Format PROC-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /processes/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution).
decisions.rationale: PROC reads unambiguously as 'process' and avoids the genuine readability problem with PRC. The three-letter candidate PRC reads as a typo or an obscure acronym; consultants would have to mentally re-expand it every time. PR collides with public relations (which a CRM context might legitimately encounter as a domain or department) and is only two letters anyway. P is too short. PROC has no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT). The four-letter form invokes domain.md's explicit deviation clause; no precedent is broken.
decisions.alternatives_considered: PRC (three letters, reads as typo, rejected). PR (two letters, collides with public relations, rejected). P (one letter, too short, rejected). PROCESS (seven letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with other prefixes, rejected).
decisions.consequences: process.md uses PROC-NNN throughout. No collision with existing prefixes. The crm_candidate spec conversation retains the soft-3-letter posture's flexibility-budget and decides its own prefix in its own section 3.1 (working assumption from SES-011 workstream plan was CRM but is potentially ambiguous against product-name; may end up as CRMC or similar). Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.

decisions.title: process field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope, with no-status-field deviation and engagement-global name uniqueness
decisions.context: The process methodology entity must host the Phase 1 Prioritized Backbone output (section 7.3 of phase-1-interview-guide.md): per process, a client-language name, a one-sentence purpose, a domain affiliation, a priority classification, the reasoning behind the classification, and the handoffs to/from other backbone processes. Phase 1 explicitly does not produce detailed process definitions; that's Phase 3 territory deferred under PI-005. The minimum-viable v0.4 schema captures only what Phase 1 produces.
decisions.decision: Adopt a thin field inventory: process_identifier, process_name (required, engagement-global case-insensitive unique), process_domain_identifier (required, direct FK to domain.domain_identifier), process_purpose (required, one-sentence), process_classification_rationale (optional, classification reasoning text), process_notes (optional, internal scratchpad), process_classification (separate decision, see DEC-057), plus inherited timestamps. No separate process_description (one purpose field carries the work-content meaning for process the way two fields don't need to). No process_kind / base-type classification. No storage-level length caps. No process_status field — a deviation from domain.md / entity.md precedent — because the methodology's lifecycle on process is about definitional completeness, not propose-verify scope, and v0.4 schema has only the identified level (a status field with one value is no field at all). The engagement-scope information that domain and entity carry via status=deferred is carried for process by process_classification=deferred instead.
decisions.rationale: Engagement-global name uniqueness was initially proposed as per-(domain, name) unique to allow natural client-language names like 'Recruit' in both Mentor Recruitment and Client Recruiting domains. The user pushed back applying the principle 'if there could ever be user confusion, then we should go engagement specific' — process handoff lists in Phase 1 output documents reference process names alone without domain context, creating ambiguity risk under per-domain uniqueness. Engagement-global mirrors precedent and matches CBM's existing pattern of suffixing names (Mentor Recruit, Client Recruit). The no-status-field deviation rationale: domain and entity use status to track propose-verify engagement scope; process has process_classification serving exactly that role (a process with classification=deferred is acknowledged-but-out-of-current-scope, same semantic as status=deferred for domain/entity). Adding a separate status field would either duplicate the meaning or introduce field-collision ambiguity (which 'deferred' applies?). A definitional-completeness lifecycle field (working name process_definition_level) returns in v0.5+ when PI-005 introduces detailed-process schema content with multiple completeness levels distinguishable in the schema. Single-content-field choice (purpose, no separate description) follows the same reasoning entity.md applied — for process, both questions reduce to 'what does this process do?' and one field handles it without information loss.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Per-(domain, name) name uniqueness (rejected for downstream documentation rendering ambiguity). Two-content-field split mirroring domain (purpose + description, rejected because for process both questions collapse to one). Three-value status field mirroring domain/entity (rejected because the methodology's process lifecycle is definitional, not propose-verify, and v0.4 has only one definitional level). Conditional-required process_classification_rationale (rejected as schema complexity; optional with UI placeholder cuing is simpler).
decisions.consequences: process.md schema in v0.4 has eight substantive fields plus inherited timestamps. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies the deviation rationale is acceptable (it is — the spec guide section 6 permits deviation with explicit justification, and the deviation is explicitly justified). When v0.5+ introduces process schema growth (PI-005), a definitional-completeness lifecycle field returns. Future scalar implementation-priority is tracked separately as PI-011.

decisions.title: process_classification field with four-value enum and unclassified default
decisions.context: Principle 3 of the evolved methodology says priority is established at the process level and inherited downward; mission-criticality is the consistent organizing principle. Phase 1 produces three priority buckets in its Prioritized Backbone output: Backbone (mission-critical), Supporting, Deferred. Between Session 1 (process names captured from client conversation) and the between-session drafting work that classifies them, processes exist in an unclassified state — the Phase 1 guide explicitly says 'do not yet propose mission-critical classification — that's between-session work' (line 237).
decisions.decision: Adopt process_classification as the field name (not process_priority — the user identified the methodology's three-bucket classification as conceptually distinct from a conventional scalar priority and pushed back on the priority terminology). Enum values: unclassified (default starter), mission_critical, supporting, deferred. Transitions: one-way out of unclassified (no regression to unclassified once classified); free bidirectional movement among the three classified values per Principle 7 ('decisions inside shipped iterations are locked; priority is not'). Server-side classification-transition validation per section 3.5.3 returns HTTP 422 on invalid transitions with {"error": "invalid_classification_transition", "from": ..., "to": ...}.
decisions.rationale: Field naming: the user observed that conventional priority semantics imply a scalar (Critical/High/Medium/Low) ranking, whereas mission-critical/supporting/deferred is a classification fused with scope decisions and role descriptions. Calling the field 'priority' would misrepresent its semantic. 'classification' is more accurate and matches the methodology's actual semantic (a methodology classification fused with engagement-scope information). Default unclassified reflects the Phase 1 cadence faithfully — Session 1 captures names without classifying; between-session work classifies; Session 2 verifies the classifications. Making classification required-on-create would either block Session 1 capture or force premature pattern-matching. One-way out of unclassified protects the classification work as a meaningful engagement event; demoting a classified process should change to deferred rather than regress to unclassified. Free movement among classified values supports Principle 7's explicit allowance for cross-iteration priority review.
decisions.alternatives_considered: process_priority as field name (rejected — semantically misleading per user feedback). process_priority with values Critical/High/Medium/Low replacing the three-bucket classification (rejected — would diverge from Phase 1 7.3 output specification which explicitly produces Backbone / Supporting / Deferred sections by name; methodology change should precede schema change). process_tier as field name (acceptable alternative but classification is closer to the methodology's actual term). Required-no-default classification (rejected — incompatible with Phase 1 Session 1 capture cadence). Free regression to unclassified after classification (rejected — same rationale as domain's one-way propose-verify gate: post-decision state is meaningful and should be re-expressed via the classified values rather than abandoned).
decisions.consequences: process records start unclassified by default; consultants classify between Session 1 and Session 2. The Phase 1 output document at end of Session 2 has zero unclassified processes (or any remaining unclassified are flagged as work-in-progress). A future scalar implementation-priority field (PI-011) attaches separately when Phase 2 Slice Planning content lands in v0.5+; this field carries methodology classification, that one carries implementation ranking. Cross-spec consistency check confirms process is the only methodology entity with a classification field in v0.4 (domain and entity have no analog).

decisions.title: process relationship architecture: direct FK for domain, references-entity directional edge for handoffs, defer process-to-entity entirely
decisions.context: Process is the most relational of the four methodology entity types. It belongs to one domain (many-to-one), connects to other process records through workflow handoffs (many-to-many), and is anticipated to touch entity records in v0.5+ once Phase 3 work demands. The mechanism for each connection (direct FK column vs references-entity edge) is decided per the spec guide section 3.3 case framework: direct FK when cardinality is settled and there's no edge metadata; references-entity edge when many-to-many or when metadata attaches to the edge.
decisions.decision: Three architectural sub-decisions: (1) Domain affiliation via direct FK column (process_domain_identifier on the processes table). Many-to-one cardinality is settled at the methodology level (a process belongs to exactly one domain — Phase 1's Prioritized Backbone groups processes by domain). No vocab.py registration because the references vocab governs the references table only and direct FK doesn't produce references rows. (2) Process-to-process handoffs via references-entity edge with new vocabulary kind process_hands_off_to_process. Directional (source = producer, target = consumer) reflecting Phase 1 guide section 4.1 language: 'Process A hands off to Process B if A produces records or state that B requires to do its work.' Many-to-many cardinality (each process can hand off to many and receive from many). Self-loops technically valid but unusual. Bidirectional handoffs (feedback loops) require two distinct edges (A → B and B → A); the references uniqueness constraint prevents duplicates of identical (source, target, kind) tuples. (3) Process-to-entity touches deferred entirely. No vocab registration in v0.4. The anticipated v0.5+ kind (process_touches_entity, possibly decomposed into read/create/update/delete variants) lists informationally in section 3.3.2 but is not added to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS or _kinds_for_pair. Phase 1 does not formally produce this data; Phase 3 may need finer-grained kinds that a pre-committed coarse name would ossify.
decisions.rationale: Domain FK choice: cardinality is settled (per the methodology), edge has no metadata, lookup is trivial via FK. References-entity edge would be overkill and inconsistent with entity.md's many-to-many entity_scopes_to_domain only because the underlying cardinalities differ. Directional process-to-process: methodology language is inherently directional ('hands off'); Phase 1 output (section 7.3) names both directions distinctly per process ('handoffs to/from other backbone processes'). Undirected representation would force the consultant to track direction outside the system and force renderers to derive direction at display time. Cost of directional: feedback loops require two edges, but feedback loops are rare in Phase 1 backbones. Process-to-entity defer rationale: shipping a vocab name without Phase 3's actual semantic detail is a worse bet than waiting; PI-005 already tracks the work. Cost of defer: consultants who want to capture entity-touch data during Phase 1 fall back to freeform notes in process_notes, but Phase 1's official output does not require touches so the friction is small.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Domain via references-entity edge with new vocab kind (rejected — references mechanism is for many-to-many, not many-to-one with settled cardinality). Process-to-process undirected (rejected — methodology language is directional and would create rendering ambiguity). Process-to-process named process_connects_to_process or process_feeds_process (rejected — hands_off_to matches the methodology guide's literal word). Pre-register process_touches_entity in v0.4 with no UI to create the edges (rejected — risks ossifying the wrong name when Phase 3 may need read/create/update/delete decomposition). Pre-register finer-grained process_reads_entity / process_creates_entity / process_updates_entity / process_deletes_entity in v0.4 (rejected — premature commitment without Phase 3 design work).
decisions.consequences: process table has process_domain_identifier FK column; processes always belong to exactly one live domain. process_hands_off_to_process registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and _kinds_for_pair; Alembic migration extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint. process_belongs_to_domain stays a conceptual relationship name only — referenced informationally but not in vocab. Process-to-entity vocab waits for v0.5+ work under PI-005. Cross-spec consistency check confirms no name collision between process_hands_off_to_process and entity_scopes_to_domain (clearly distinct).

decisions.title: process API surface, UI defaults, and acceptance criteria for v0.4
decisions.context: The process methodology entity needs a REST API surface, a desktop UI panel, and concrete acceptance criteria for v0.4 build. Defaults from domain.md and entity.md are honored unless deviation is methodology-driven; the spec guide section 3.5 (API), 3.6 (UI), and 3.7 (acceptance criteria) provide the template.
decisions.decision: Standard endpoint set: GET /processes (with ?include_deleted=true), GET /processes/{identifier}, POST /processes, PUT /processes/{identifier}, PATCH /processes/{identifier}, DELETE /processes/{identifier} (soft-delete), POST /processes/{identifier}/restore, GET /processes/next-identifier. No deviations. Server-side classification-transition validation per section 3.4.2; server-side domain-FK validation rejecting references to non-existent or soft-deleted domains. Decomposed reference handling for handoffs: separate POST /references calls with source_type=process, target_type=process, relationship_kind=process_hands_off_to_process. No inline-handoff convenience endpoint. UI: Methodology sidebar group position #3 (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates — all four ship together in v0.4). Master pane columns Identifier / Name / Classification / Updated, sorted by Identifier ascending. Domain column deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes, mirroring entity.md's deferral posture (PI-009 tracks the entity-side column; if the CBM redo reveals the Processes panel needs an analogous column, a follow-on PI is authored at that time). Detail pane vertical layout with fields in section 3.2 order; ReferencesSection renders bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process references in separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections. Create-dialog handoff flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as a v0.4-build decision matching the parallel open question on entity affiliations. 15 testable acceptance criteria cover migration, validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoints, identifier helper, soft-delete/restore, sidebar position, master pane, detail pane, CRUD dialogs, file-watch, vocab registration, references round-trip, sample CBM Phase 1 backbone authoring.
decisions.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity. Server-side validation (classification-transition, domain-FK) prevents UI-only enforcement bugs from corrupting engagement-scope state. Decomposed reference handling keeps the API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches the entity.md / domain.md precedent. UI sidebar position #3 follows workstream order. Master pane Domain-column deferral mirrors entity.md (DEC-054) — without short codes the column renders DOM-NNN identifiers that don't tell a consultant anything at a glance, and the batched-join query is design work not yet validated against real-engagement signal. Detail pane separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections honor the directional semantics of process_hands_off_to_process — collapsing them into a single 'Related processes' list would lose the direction information consultants need to read the backbone correctly. Create-dialog handoff flow open question matches entity.md's posture rather than locking the choice without v0.4-build coordination on parallel UX.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Bulk-create endpoint (rejected — not in scope for v0.4; case for it has not surfaced). Inline-handoff convenience endpoint (rejected — diverges from v2's decomposed-reference posture). Include Domain column in v0.4 master pane (rejected — would render DOM-NNN identifiers; same posture as entity.md). Collapse Hands-off-to and Receives-from into one references sub-section (rejected — loses directional information). Lock create-dialog handoff flow now (rejected — UI-layer decision better coordinated with parallel entity decision at v0.4-build planning).
decisions.consequences: process API surface is implementable without surprises against the v0.3 governance-entity patterns. UI is implementable as a ListDetailPanel subclass with the standard CRUD-dialog suite plus a ReferencesSection rendering bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process edges in two named sub-sections. Acceptance criteria #10 through #15 give the v0.4 build concrete UI tests. The Domain-column deferral creates one v0.5+ follow-on PI (parallel to PI-009 but for the Processes panel) if CBM redo signal warrants — not authored now to avoid premature PI proliferation. Create-dialog handoff flow open question gets resolved at v0.4-build planning, coordinated with the parallel entity affiliations question.

planning_items.title: Future scalar implementation-priority field on process for ranking within classification buckets
planning_items.description: DEC-057 adopted process_classification (mission_critical / supporting / deferred / unclassified) as the methodology priority classification per Principle 3. During the conversation the user observed that this is a classification, not a conventional scalar priority, and the methodology's three buckets don't answer the implementation-ranking question — 'of the mission-critical processes, which do we build first?'. A separate scalar implementation-priority field (working name process_priority, values Critical / High / Medium / Low or 1-5 or similar) is deferred to v0.5+. The deferral reasoning: Phase 1 does not formally produce per-process implementation priority — that information emerges from Phase 2 Slice Planning, which v0.4 schema does not yet host as content. When Phase 2 (Slice Planning) iteration content lands in v0.5+, the scalar priority field is part of that work, scoped per-iteration rather than global to the process record (the same process might have different scalar priorities in different iterations). Implementation when this PI lands: (a) add process_priority field with chosen value space; (b) decide whether the field is global on the process record or per-iteration via a join table to a future Iteration entity; (c) UI: master-pane column showing classification + priority paired (e.g., 'mission_critical / High'); (d) priority-transition validation if any (probably free movement per Principle 7). Prioritization signal: CBM redo Phase 1 will reveal whether consultants want implementation-priority ranking at Phase 1 cadence (probably no — Phase 1 outputs classification, not implementation order). Real signal comes from Phase 2 when slice planning content needs to host iteration-membership decisions.
